mission.title: Install and Configure Linkerd on Kubernetes
mission.description: Linkerd is an ultralight, security-first service mesh for Kubernetes that enhances observability, reliability, and security for your microservices without requiring code changes. Linkerd requires two separate Helm charts (linkerd-crds and linkerd-control-plane) and TLS certificates for the identity system.

steps.title: Generate TLS certificates
steps.description:
Linkerd requires a trust anchor certificate and issuer credentials for its identity system. Generate them using step CLI:

```bash
step certificate create root.linkerd.cluster.local ca.crt ca.key \
  --profile root-ca --no-password --insecure

step certificate create identity.linkerd.cluster.local issuer.crt issuer.key \
  --profile intermediate-ca --not-after 8760h --no-password --insecure \
  --ca ca.crt --ca-key ca.key
```

Alternatively, use openssl or cert-manager to generate the certificates.

steps.title: Add the Linkerd Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Linkerd Helm repository:

```bash
helm repo add linkerd-edge https://helm.linkerd.io/edge
helm repo update
```

For stable releases, use:
```bash
helm repo add linkerd-stable https://helm.linkerd.io/stable
```

steps.title: Install Linkerd CRDs
steps.description:
Install the Linkerd Custom Resource Definitions:

```bash
helm install linkerd-crds linkerd-edge/linkerd-crds \
  -n linkerd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Install Linkerd control plane
steps.description:
Install the Linkerd control plane with the TLS certificates:

```bash
helm install linkerd-control-plane linkerd-edge/linkerd-control-plane \
  -n linkerd \
  --set-file identityTrustAnchorsPEM=ca.crt \
  --set-file identity.issuer.tls.crtPEM=issuer.crt \
  --set-file identity.issuer.tls.keyPEM=issuer.key
```

**Alternative: Install using the linkerd CLI**
```bash
linkerd install --crds | kubectl apply -f -
linkerd install | kubectl apply -f -
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Linkerd control plane pods are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd
```

Or use the linkerd CLI for a comprehensive check:

```bash
linkerd check
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Linkerd control plane
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the control plane Helm release:

```bash
helm uninstall linkerd-control-plane -n linkerd
```

uninstall.title: Remove Linkerd CRDs
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CRDs Helm release:

```bash
helm uninstall linkerd-crds -n linkerd
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Linkerd namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Linkerd namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace linkerd
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Linkerd
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade both charts in order:

```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade linkerd-crds linkerd-edge/linkerd-crds -n linkerd
helm upgrade linkerd-control-plane linkerd-edge/linkerd-control-plane -n linkerd \
  --set-file identityTrustAnchorsPEM=ca.crt \
  --set-file identity.issuer.tls.crtPEM=issuer.crt \
  --set-file identity.issuer.tls.keyPEM=issuer.key
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded components are running:

```bash
linkerd check
kubectl get pods -n linkerd
```

troubleshooting.title: Identity certificate errors
troubleshooting.description:
If pods fail with identity/TLS errors, verify your certificates are valid and not expired:

```bash
linkerd check --proxy
openssl x509 -in ca.crt -noout -dates
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the failing pod:

```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n linkerd
```

troubleshooting.title: linkerd check fails
troubleshooting.description:
Run linkerd check with verbose output to diagnose issues:

```bash
linkerd check --verbose
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Linkerd will have all control plane pods (destination, identity, proxy-injector) running in the linkerd namespace, and linkerd check will pass all checks. You can then inject the Linkerd proxy into workloads by annotating namespaces or deployments.
codeSnippets: helm install linkerd-control-plane linkerd-edge/linkerd-control-plane -n linkerd --set-file identityTrustAnchorsPEM=ca.crt --set-file identity.issuer.tls.crtPEM=issuer.crt --set-file identity.issuer.tls.keyPEM=issuer.key

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster, Helm v3, kubectl, and a tool to generate TLS certificates (step CLI recommended, or openssl). The linkerd CLI is optional but recommended for verification.
